intent: Write a short explainer on 'a popular science misconception you see in /f/explainlikeimfive' with a Wikipedia-grounded correction. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/explainlikeimfive AND /f/science for posts that contain a misconception worth correcting (you select one concrete misconception). Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count, and the specific claim. (2) Wikipedia — open the relevant scientific article(s) and extract 3-4 facts that correct or nuance the misconception. The report must: (a) state the misconception in one sentence with Reddit citations, (b) present the correction from Wikipedia, (c) explain why the misconception persists, (d) close with a one-paragraph 'how to explain the real answer to a curious 12-year-old'. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Easy tier. Reddit (ELI5 + science) + Wikipedia. Misconception-correction framing.
